# Kubernetes OOM Troubleshooting

When a pod is OOMKilled, inspect memory usage and check for memory leaks. | Parameter | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| limit | 1Gi |
| request | 512Mi |

```bash
kubectl describe pod api-gateway
kubectl top pod api-gateway
```

This file tests:
- Markdown parsing
- Table detection
- Code block detection

TICKET ID: TICK-001

TITLE: Kubernetes pod OOMKilled in production namespace

DESCRIPTION:
Pod 'api-gateway-7d4f9c' in namespace 'production' is being repeatedly OOMKilled. Memory limit set to 512Mi but container is consuming up to 700Mi during peak load. This began after the v2.4.1 deployment on 2024 10. Symptoms: intermittent 503 errors from downstream services, latency spikes to 8s. RESOLUTION:
Increased memory limit to 1Gi and requests to 512Mi in the Deployment manifest. Also identified a memory leak in the connection pool of the Redis client library (jedis v3.6.0). Upgraded to jedis v3.8.0 which patches CVE XXXX. Applied HPA with minReplicas=2 to prevent single-pod overload. TAGS: kubernetes, oom, memory-leak, redis, api-gateway, production

TICKET ID: TICK-002

TITLE: CI/CD pipeline fails on Docker build step — layer cache miss

DESCRIPTION:
Jenkins pipeline for the 'data-service' repo fails consistently at the 'docker build' stage. Error: 'failed to solve: failed to read dockerfile: open /var/lib/docker/tmp/buildkit-XXX/Dockerfile: no such file or directory'. Issue started after migrating Jenkins agents to new EC2 instance type (c6i.2xlarge). Build time has increased from 4 minutes to 22 minutes. RESOLUTION:
Root cause: BuildKit daemon was not enabled on the new AMI. Added DOCKER_BUILDKIT=1 to the Jenkins agent environment variables. Enabled layer caching by mounting /var/lib/docker as a persistent EBS volume. Build time restored to ~4.5 minutes. Added .dockerignore to exclude node_modules and .git from build context. TAGS: ci-cd, jenkins, docker, buildkit, cache, ec2

TICKET ID: TICK-003

TITLE: Database connection pool exhaustion under high load — PostgreSQL

DESCRIPTION:
The 'orders-service' is throwing 'FATAL: remaining connection slots are reserved for non-replication superuser connections' during Black Friday load test. Max connections on RDS PostgreSQL instance is 200 but pgAdmin shows 198 connections all in 'idle' state. Service uses HikariCP with default settings (maximumPoolSize=10, minimumIdle=10) across 20 pod replicas. RESOLUTION:
Deployed PgBouncer as a connection pooler in 'transaction' mode. Configured pool_size=50 per service with max_client_conn=500. Reduced HikariCP maximumPoolSize to 5 per pod (20 pods * 5 = 100 connections to PgBouncer, PgBouncer multiplexes to 50 actual DB connections). Idle connections dropped from 198 to 12. Added connection leak detection with leakDetectionThreshold=5000. TAGS: postgresql, connection-pool, hikaricp, pgbouncer, orders-service, performance

TICKET ID: TICK-004

TITLE: Grafana dashboard shows missing metrics after Prometheus scrape interval change

DESCRIPTION:
After changing Prometheus global scrape_interval from 15s to 60s to reduce storage costs, several Grafana dashboards show 'No data' for panels using rate() and irate() functions. Affected dashboards: API Latency Overview, Error Rate SLO, Infrastructure Cost. Impact: SRE team is blind to real-time anomalies. RESOLUTION:
The rate() function requires at least 2 data points within the time window. With scrape_interval=60s, the default rate window of [1m] only captures 1 point. Solution: Updated all rate() calls to use [5m] window (e.g., rate(http_requests_total[5m])). Also added recording rules in Prometheus to pre-compute expensive queries. Dashboards restored. Long-term: consider VictoriaMetrics for cost-efficient high-cardinality metrics. TAGS: prometheus, grafana, monitoring, rate-function, scrape-interval, sre
Redis connection pools can exhaust database connections under high load. PgBouncer multiplexes client connections and reduces idle sessions. Connection leak detection should be enabled in HikariCP.

# Team Name: Runtime Terror

# Use Case Name: Enterprise Knowledge Copilot (RAG + Agentic Workflow)

- Anshika Shrivastava - anshikashrivastava2012@gmail.com
- Rhythm Gandhi - rhythmgandhi2005@gmail.com
- Yash Pratap Singh - yashpratap1837@gmail.com
- Arpan Mathur - arpanmathur18@gmail.com
- Kavyansh Vats - kavyansh1509@gmail.com
- Mohit Upadhyay - mohit29025@gmail.com

# Problem understanding and objective

# 1. Problem Statement

Large IT enterprises face "Knowledge Fragmentation", where insights are trapped in scattered PDFs, silos, and duplicate tickets. This lack of unified intelligence forces engineers to waste ~20% of their time searching, leading to operational delays, repetitive errors, and inflated onboarding costs. # 2. Objective & Stakeholders

To build an Agentic Copilot that transforms static data into a reasoning-capable AI Engineer. - Engineers: Instant, source-backed technical solutions. - Support Teams: Massive reduction in L1/L2 ticket volume. - Management: Faster deployment cycles and data-driven talent scaling. # 3. The Dire Need

Traditional search is obsolete for modern, complex architectures (Kubernetes/Cloud). As systems scale, the "cost of not knowing" grows. Companies need a solution that doesn't just retrieve text but understands relationships to prevent critical downtime. # 4. Social & Organizational Impact

- Democratizes Expertise: Empowers junior developers to perform at a senior level via AI-mentorship. - Reduces Burnout: Eliminates information overload and search-fatigue. - Inclusive Growth: Provides high-tier technical guidance to underprivileged talent entering the workforce.

# Market size and business impact

How much is inefficient knowledge access really costing enterprises?

# The Economic Drain

- The 30% Productivity Gap: Knowledge workers waste 2.5 hours daily searching for fragmented info.
- The Talent Leak: IT firms lose $15K–$20K per employee annually in lost productivity.
- Industry Impact: A staggering $15B annual loss across global IT services due to information silos.

# Market Opportunity (2026-2035)

- The Shift: Market projected to grow from $931B to $4.2T as firms ditch static wikis for AI agents.
- The CAGR: 27.4% growth in AI Copilots, signaling a massive enterprise transition.

# Our ROI

- Resolution Speed: 40–60% reduction in support ticket resolution time.
- Engineer Onboarding: Ramp-up time cut from months to days via AI-mentorship.
- Direct Savings: Projected $200K+ annual saving for every 100-engineer team.

# Financial Viability

- Lean Execution: MVP ready in 4–6 weeks with a minimal PoC investment.
- Zero Licensing: Built on Open Source (LangChain, ChromaDB) to bypass expensive SaaS fees.

| Enterprise AI Agent Market Growth (USD Billions) | |
| ------------------------------------------------ | ----------- |
| Year | Market Size |
| 2024 | |
| 2026 | |
| 2028 | |

| 2034 | |

-27.4% CAGR

# Existing Solutions in the market

# Why are $100B Enterprises still struggling with "Search"? # 1. Current State & Shortcomings

Static Wikis & Tribal Knowledge: Teams rely on "Dead Documentation" (SharePoint/Wikis) that is obsolete the moment it's written. Expertise is trapped in unindexed Slack/Teams chats. The "Keyword" Tax: Traditional search requires users to know exactly what to look for. If you don't use the perfect term, the information remains invisible. Information Sprawl: Knowledge is fragmented across PDFs, SOPs, and tickets, leading to a 20–30% productivity drain purely on "information hunting."

# 2. The Competition & The "Context Gap"

MS Copilot / AWS Q: While powerful, they are "Broad but Shallow". They lack the deep, private logic of your specific legacy systems and internal "hacks."

Standard RAG Tools: Most tools (Glean/Guru) are Passive Chatbots. They return flat text chunks but lack the Reasoning Layer needed to actually diagnose a system failure. The Hallucination Risk: Generic bots often "guess" technical steps when data is missing, creating a dangerous Trust Gap in high-stakes IT environments. # 3. Our Differentiators

GraphRAG++: We move from "Flat Chunks" to "Relational Logic". By using a Knowledge Graph, We understand that Error X in Service A is actually caused by Database B. Self-Learning Feedback Loop: Our system isn't frozen. It "listens" to every newly resolved ticket and user "Thumbs Up," evolving its intelligence in real-time. Confidence-Gated Escalation: We solve the trust issue with a "Trust Score". If the AI isn't 90% sure, it doesn't guess, it summarizes the case and hands it off to a human expert.

# Proposed solution approach

“From static knowledge retrieval → to intelligent reasoning, learning, and action.”

# Solution Approach & High-Level Design

We utilize a Hybrid Graph-RAG & Agentic Workflow. Instead of simple text retrieval, it models enterprise data as an interconnected Knowledge Graph. # Ingestion

Scrapes and chunks PDFs, Wikis, and Tickets using LlamaParse for high-fidelity table/chart extraction. # Orchestration

Employs a Multi-Agent ReAct pattern (via LangChain/LangGraph) to plan, search, and execute diagnostic tasks. # Storage

Dual-database approach—ChromaDB for vector similarity and Neo4j for relational reasoning. # Our Winning USP (Unique Selling Proposition)

Relational Reasoning (GraphRAG): Unlike competitors who fetch "flat" text. The Self-Learning Feedback Loop: Every resolved ticket and human-verified answer is re-indexed, meaning the system gets smarter with every interaction. Trust-Gated Escalation: Features a unique Confidence Engine. If the AI is unsure, it drafts a detailed hand-off for a human engineer instead of hallucinating. # Dependencies & Assumptions

Data Access: Assumes secure read-access to internal documentation repositories (Confluence/SharePoint/Jira). Compute: Requires high-performance LLM API access (GPT-4o/Claude 3.5) or local inference for sensitive data. Human-in-the-Loop: Effectiveness relies on initial expert verification to "seed" the self-learning loop with high-quality resolutions.

# Proposed solution approach

# 1. Understands entities and their relationships

Relational Reasoning (GraphRAG)

Connects systems, services, errors, causes & fixes

Unlike competitors who fetch "flat" text. Delivers context-aware answers, not just keyword matches

# 2. Self-Learning Feedback Loop

Every resolved ticket is captured

Human-verified answers are re-indexed

Gets smarter with every interaction. Continuously improves accuracy and relevance

# 3. Trust-Gated Escalation

Confidence Engine evaluates answer certainty

High confidence (≥ 90%) → answer with sources

Low confidence (< 90%) → structured hand-off to human engineer

# Technical Details

# Technical Feasibility

- Modular Open-Source Stack: Leveraging LangChain and FastAPI ensures we aren't "reinventing the wheel." These are industry-standard, well-documented frameworks. - Model Agnostic Design: Our architecture works with OpenAI, Anthropic, or local models like Llama 3, allowing for flexible deployment based on the client's existing infrastructure. - Proven RAG Patterns: We utilize Hybrid Search (Vector + Keyword) and Reranking, which are established methods for high-accuracy information retrieval. # Business Viability

- High ROI / Low OpEx: By using open-source vector databases (ChromaDB/Qdrant) and graph databases (Neo4j), we eliminate recurring per-user licensing costs associated with proprietary SaaS. - Reduced MTTR: The system directly addresses the "Time-to-Resolution" KPI, which is the primary driver of cost in IT Service Management (ITSM). - Seamless Integration: Designed to plug into existing enterprise ecosystems (Slack, Microsoft Teams, Jira) without disrupting established workflows.

Design Considerations: Security & Scalability

# 1. Security & Data Integrity

- PII Masking: Automated redaction layers ensure GDPR compliance before data reaches the LLM. - Enterprise RBAC: Inherits existing permissions; access is restricted based on organizational hierarchy. - VPC Deployment: Option for Private Cloud hosting to guarantee zero data leakage to public providers. # 2. Scalability & Performance

- Async Engine: Powered by FastAPI & Celery to handle thousands of concurrent queries without latency. - Distributed Vector DB: Uses Qdrant to maintain sub-second retrieval as data grows to millions of docs. - K8s Ready: Fully containerized Microservices (Docker) for seamless horizontal scaling. # 3. Technical Complexity & Validation: “The Engineering Challenge”

- Agentic Orchestration: Managing complex multi-step reasoning and state transitions via LangGraph. - GraphRAG Synergy: Synchronizing Neo4j (Relational) with Vector DB (Semantic) for a 360° knowledge view. - Real-time Synchronization: Ensuring the Knowledge Graph stays updated as new tickets are resolved. # 4. The "Gold Standard" Evaluation

- RAGAS Framework: We move beyond "vibe-based" testing to mathematical scoring of Faithfulness, Relevancy, and Context Precision. - Continuous Monitoring: Automated feedback loops to detect and eliminate hallucinations in production.

# Technical Details

# SOLUTION APPROACH & HIGH-LEVEL DESIGN

From static knowledge retrieval → to intelligent reasoning, learning & action

| 1. INGEST | 2. PROCESS | 3. ORCHESTRATE | 4. STORE (HYBRID) | 5. RESPOND |
| -------------------------- | ------------------------ | -------------------------- | ------------------------------ | --------------------------------- |
| Unify enterprise knowledge | Extract & structure | Multi-Agent ReAct Workflow | Dual DB for deep understanding | Trust-gated output |
| PDFs | User Query | ChromaDB | High Confidence (≥ 90%) | Answer with Sources |
| Wikis | | Semantic Search | | |
| Tickets | LlamaParse | Search Agent | Orchestrator | Tool Agent |
| | High-fidelity extraction | (Find) | (Plan) | (Execute) |
| Chats / Logs | (Tables, Charts, Logs) | | Neo4j | Low Confidence (< 90%) |
| Reasoning Agent | | | Relational Reasoning | Escalate with Structured Hand-off |

# OUTPUT

- Context-aware Answer (with Sources)
- Step-by-step Resolution
- Related Tickets & Docs
- Confidence Score
